title: Delicate Juicy Moroccan Fish Pastilla
description: A traditional Moroccan dish featuring white fish, onions, and spices wrapped in a flaky pastry, perfect for a family dinner. This recipe is kid-friendly and pescatarian-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, saute the chopped onions and minced garlic until softened.
Add the white fish fillets to the skillet and cook until opaque and flaky.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked fish, sauteed onions and garlic, salt, black pepper, cumin, and paprika.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the white flour and melted butter to form a pastry dough.
Roll out the pastry dough to a large square, about 1/8 inch thick.
Place the fish mixture in the center of the pastry square, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the pastry with the beaten egg.
Fold the pastry over the fish mixture to form a triangle or square shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Place the pastilla on a baking sheet and brush the top with the remaining egg.
Bake the pastilla in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
